config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena kalau tidak, mudah sekali disalahgunakan oleh negara atau mayoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan bagi setiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dijaga, bukan dari individu yang terus dikalahkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can become an excuse for government overreach or majority abuse. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case: public health, safety, and basic order sometimes justify limits, but those limits need to be narrow, transparent, and accountable. The real test is whether society is protecting people without crushing dissent, minority rights, or personal liberty. If the balance tips too far toward society, you stop having a free society and start having a system that simply tells people to comply.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text:
Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, diskriminasi minoritas, atau keputusan yang mengorbankan orang kecil.  

Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik, bencana, atau keamanan yang menuntut pembatasan hak, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang lebih baik, melainkan mayoritas yang makin leluasa menekan yang lemah.  

Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir ketika hak individu dilindungi kuat, karena dari sanalah kepercayaan, partisipasi, dan tanggung jawab sosial tumbuh.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to justify overreach, censorship, and mistreatment of unpopular groups. Public health, safety, and emergency needs can justify some limits, but those limits have to be narrow, evidence-based, and checked by real accountability, not just vague appeals to the common good. I also do not buy the idea that protecting individual rights is somehow anti-social; in my view, a society is stronger when people trust that their rights will not be sacrificed whenever the majority gets impatient. When “society” becomes a blank check, the people with the least power usually pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: di Indonesia, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dijadikan alasan untuk menekan kelompok kecil dan mengabaikan hak orang per orang. Argumen dari Agent B soal pembatasan yang sempit dan akuntabel menurut saya tepat, karena tanpa itu istilah kepentingan umum gampang berubah jadi alat pembenar bagi mayoritas atau negara. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling sering mengorbankan individu, tetapi yang berani melindungi hak individu bahkan saat itu tidak populer. Pembatasan boleh ada, tapi harus benar-benar pengecualian, bukan prinsip utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society’s interests the default priority over individual rights, because in the United States that has too often been the language used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. At the same time, I do not see individual rights as unlimited; public health, safety, and emergency situations can justify narrow limits, but only with strong evidence and real accountability. What I reject is the idea that “society” gets a blank check, because the people who lose out first are usually minorities, dissenters, and anyone without power. A healthy society protects the individual first precisely so the common good cannot be used as a weapon against them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
